original_claim: Dr. John Dwyer of Royal Adelaide Hospital was the lead pathologist in the original autopsy.
recommended_revision: Revise to: Dr John Matthew Dwyer gave postmortem/autopsy evidence at the 1949 inquest.
reason: p.37 names John Matthew Dwyer and his medical evidence; source supports Dwyer as key postmortem witness but not the wording "lead pathologist" as such.

original_claim: The original findings included congested liver, spleen, brain, and kidneys.
recommended_revision: Revise only if exact organ list differs after official-copy review.
reason: Dwyer evidence records extensive congestion including liver, spleen and brain; kidney wording requires page-level confirmation but organs are discussed in the same medical evidence.

original_claim: The stomach was full of blood and food and was interpreted as consistent with poisoning.
recommended_revision: Revise to separate observed stomach contents from interpretation about poisoning.
reason: Inquest records a deeply congested stomach and blood mixed with food. Poison was considered, but analyst evidence found no common poison and one expert view favored natural causes over poisoning.

original_claim: No visible injuries or trauma were observed.
recommended_revision: Revise to: no decisive traumatic cause was recorded in reviewed inquest evidence.
reason: Available OCR supports no obvious violent death evidence in reviewed inquest passages, but the exact phrase no visible injuries or trauma was not located as a standalone finding.

original_claim: No alcohol was detected.
recommended_revision: Do not state until toxicology report/autopsy pages are reviewed directly.
reason: No primary-source passage confirming alcohol testing was located in acquired material.

original_claim: The 1948-1949 chemical screens checked blood, urine, and tissue for barbiturates, opiates, cyanide, carbon monoxide, and other toxins.
recommended_revision: Revise to: Cowan tested submitted specimens for common poisons; list analytes only when explicitly sourced.
reason: Cowan evidence states specimens of stomach contents, liver, muscle, blood and urine were examined and common poisons tested; specific list in claim is broader than located evidence.

original_claim: The listed toxicology screens were negative.
recommended_revision: Revise to: tests for common poisons were negative.
reason: Cowan evidence says he was unable to find signs of any common poison; claim is too broad if applied to all listed toxins.

original_claim: Digitalis was suspected as a possible poison and would have been nearly undetectable with 1940s methods.
recommended_revision: Move to later forensic-literature review, not primary-source claim.
reason: Acquired primary sources reviewed do not support digitalis as specifically suspected or undetectable.

original_claim: Strophanthin was a possible poison, difficult to detect, and rare in Australia.
recommended_revision: Requires toxicology literature/source outside current primary archive.
reason: No acquired primary source supports strophanthin rarity/detectability claim.

original_claim: Aconitine, scopolamine, rare alkaloids, or exotic agents were possible causes.
recommended_revision: Keep only as hypothesis lead if supported later.
reason: No acquired primary source supports this list of possible exotic agents.

original_claim: No container, residue, or administration method was found.
recommended_revision: Revise to: reviewed inquest passages do not identify a poison container or administration method.
reason: Reviewed inquest inventory lists carried items but does not show a poison container/residue/administration method; absence is not a complete proof across all property records.

original_claim: The official cause of death was left unknown.
recommended_revision: Revise to: precise medical cause remained undetermined in available coronial material.
reason: Coroner evidence records inability to identify a specific cause while finding death not natural/probably poison; death certificate copy concerns unknown man.

original_claim: Jessica Thomson, also known as Jestyn, lived near Somerton Beach.
recommended_revision: Acquire electoral rolls/directories/police interview notes.
reason: Jetty Road extract does not by itself verify Jessica Thomson residence in relation to Somerton Beach.

original_claim: A phone number found in a Rubaiyat copy was linked to Jessica Thomson.
recommended_revision: Do not state as verified until police-file pages are manually transcribed.
reason: Primary-source reproductions likely relate to Rubaiyat/phone lead but require visual page review; no OCR-supported passage was extracted.

original_claim: Jessica Thomson denied knowing the deceased but reportedly showed visible shock during police interview.
recommended_revision: Acquire interview notes or reliable transcript.
reason: No acquired primary-source passage records Jessica Thomson interview behavior or denial.

original_claim: Robin Thomson bore a strong physical resemblance to the Somerton Man.
recommended_revision: Requires image-comparison source; avoid claim wording.
reason: No acquired primary material verifies resemblance claim.

original_claim: DNA later confirmed a partial link involving Robin Thomson.
recommended_revision: Requires FSSA/SAPOL or published genetic-genealogy evidence.
reason: No acquired primary forensic/DNA report verifies partial DNA link involving Robin Thomson.

original_claim: The victim may have held or used a Melbourne-Adelaide train ticket.
recommended_revision: Remove or mark as separate unverified lead.
reason: Acquired inquest evidence supports Henley Beach ticket and bus ticket, not a Melbourne-Adelaide train ticket.

original_claim: An unused Henley Beach train ticket was associated with the case.
recommended_revision: Revise to: an uncancelled Adelaide-to-Henley Beach railway ticket was associated with the body.
reason: Inquest records a single uncancelled railway ticket from Adelaide to Henley Beach and ticket-clerk evidence.

original_claim: There were no confirmed friends or close contacts in Adelaide.
recommended_revision: Requires police canvassing records; do not assert negative as fact.
reason: No acquired source proves absence of friends/contacts in Adelaide.

original_claim: There was no confirmed family in South Australia.
recommended_revision: Requires police identification and genealogical records.
reason: No acquired source proves absence of family in South Australia.

original_claim: No proven links existed to criminal or intelligence circles.
recommended_revision: Do not state as factual without archive search criteria.
reason: No acquired source reviews criminal/intelligence links.

original_claim: Forensic genealogists used hair from the death mask and exhumed remains in or after 2021.
recommended_revision: Requires 2021+ official/research documentation.
reason: No acquired source verifies hair from death mask/exhumed remains used by forensic genealogists.

original_claim: A family tree built from distant relatives identified Carl or Charles Webb, born 1905 in Melbourne, as the best fit.
recommended_revision: Requires DNA methodology source.
reason: Fingerprint/tooth charts do not verify DNA family-tree identification.

original_claim: Carl Webb was an electrical engineer or instrument maker.
recommended_revision: Acquire directories/electoral/employment records.
reason: Directories may help, but no acquired material verifies Webb occupation.

original_claim: Carl Webb separated from his wife around the time of disappearance.
recommended_revision: Acquire civil and court records.
reason: No divorce/marriage/court source acquired.

original_claim: Carl Webb had no known direct offspring.
recommended_revision: Acquire family tree/civil records.
reason: No genealogy source acquired.

original_claim: There was a possible indirect DNA link to Robin Thomson.
recommended_revision: Requires DNA report.
reason: No acquired primary DNA evidence supports indirect DNA link claim.

original_claim: Carl Webb's family was unaware of an Adelaide connection.
recommended_revision: Acquire interview/source statement if legally usable.
reason: No family-statement source acquired.

original_claim: Carl Webb disappeared from records after 1947.
recommended_revision: Acquire directories/electoral/civil records.
reason: No acquired records track Webb after 1947.

original_claim: Released Venona cables contain no direct reference to a Somerton-related death as of 2024.
recommended_revision: Separate intelligence archive search required.
reason: Venona corpus not acquired/reviewed in Mission 4 archive.

original_claim: Soviet cipher traffic was high in Australia after World War II.
recommended_revision: Use NAA/ASIO/Venona sources in later context review.
reason: No primary intelligence traffic source acquired.

original_claim: No declassified ASIO or MI5 document explicitly references Webb or Somerton Man by name or alias.
recommended_revision: Cannot verify a negative without search log and corpus.
reason: No comprehensive ASIO/MI5 search record acquired.

original_claim: Agent elimination and message killings occurred in other Commonwealth countries at the time.
recommended_revision: Remove from factual matrix or source separately.
reason: No acquired source verifies comparative Commonwealth message killings at the time.

original_claim: No open-source codeword, phrase, or signal is directly linked to Tamam Shud.
recommended_revision: Requires defined search corpus.
reason: No cryptographic corpus/source acquired to verify negative.

original_claim: The Rubaiyat was sometimes used as a codebook in pre-digital cryptography.
recommended_revision: Requires documented cryptographic examples.
reason: No acquired source proves Rubaiyat use as codebook in agencies.

original_claim: The deceased was middle-aged, professionally skilled, neatly dressed, clean, and well groomed.
recommended_revision: Split appearance/age from later identity/occupation claim.
reason: Inquest supports clothing of fairly good quality and middle-aged male. Professional skill is not supported by 1949 primary sources.

original_claim: Extreme care was taken to remove identifying material.
recommended_revision: Revise to avoid motive: identifying tags appeared removed; timing/actor unknown.
reason: Coroner says tags that might identify him had been removed; witness/examiner evidence notes tags appeared removed but no threads showed recent removal.

original_claim: There were no clear signs of long-term suicide planning.
recommended_revision: Analytical claim; not verified in Mission 4.
reason: No acquired source evaluates suicide planning.

original_claim: If self-administered poisoning occurred, it involved unusually high operational security.
recommended_revision: Move to analytical confidence register only after evidence review.
reason: Operational-security characterization is analytical and unsupported by acquired primary evidence.

original_claim: If externally administered, the victim may have been lured or incapacitated.
recommended_revision: Keep as hypothesis, not claim.
reason: Speculative scenario not supported by acquired primary sources.

original_claim: The body appeared posed, calm, or peaceful.
recommended_revision: Revise to body-position facts only.
reason: Inquest describes body position and witness observations; words posed/calm/peaceful are interpretive.

original_claim: The Tamam Shud slip was hidden rather than displayed.
recommended_revision: Revise to: slip was found in trouser fob pocket; do not infer intended concealment.
reason: Inquest states Tamam Shud paper was in the fob pocket and not found for some time.

original_claim: A Rubaiyat book associated with the case had a torn page and writing inside the back cover.
recommended_revision: Separate torn-page match from code-writing evidence.
reason: Inquest supports paper torn from Rubaiyat edition; code image shows letter strings. Full claim about writing inside back cover requires provenance review of image/book.

original_claim: Somerton Beach was quiet, visible, and suitable for staged discovery.
recommended_revision: Do not use as fact.
reason: Suitability for staged discovery is analytical and unsupported by acquired primary sources.

original_claim: Modern mass spectrometry could potentially detect preserved poison traces in hair, tissue, or embalmed remains.
recommended_revision: Requires forensic expert/literature source.
reason: Modern mass spectrometry possibility not addressed in acquired primary sources.

original_claim: Isotope analysis might assist with geographic origin of last meals or water source.
recommended_revision: Requires forensic expert/literature source.
reason: Isotope-analysis possibility not addressed in acquired primary sources.

original_claim: Clothing fibers, microparticles, pollen, or sand might support travel or location analysis.
recommended_revision: Requires preserved-evidence status and forensic source.
reason: Trace fiber/pollen/sand possibility not addressed in acquired primary sources.

original_claim: The Rubaiyat letter strings are too brief for ordinary frequency analysis.
recommended_revision: Requires cryptanalytic assessment.
reason: Image shows short letter strings but does not itself verify cryptanalytic frequency-analysis claim.

original_claim: The Rubaiyat writing may be a one-time pad or book cipher.
recommended_revision: Keep as hypothesis.
reason: Image does not verify one-time pad/book-cipher hypothesis.

original_claim: No solution has been accepted by cryptographic authorities as of 2024.
recommended_revision: Define authority set and source search.
reason: No corpus of cryptographic-authority statements acquired.

original_claim: The Somerton Man death in December 1948 overlapped with the Berlin Blockade.
recommended_revision: Verify from historical chronology sources later.
reason: Berlin Blockade source not acquired in Mission 4 primary archive.

original_claim: ASIO was established in March 1949.
recommended_revision: Verify in context-source matrix if needed.
reason: Official ASIO source was catalogued in Mission 2 but not part of acquired primary-source review set used here.

original_claim: Five Eyes intelligence sharing was formalized between 1948 and 1950.
recommended_revision: Requires official/scholarly source.
reason: No official Five Eyes chronology source acquired/reviewed here.

original_claim: Woomera and Maralinga were sensitive defense or atomic sites relevant to foreign intelligence interest.
recommended_revision: Verify separately with NAA/official defence records.
reason: No defence-site primary record reviewed here; Maralinga chronology especially needs care.

original_claim: Adelaide was geographically close to defense installations, research facilities, and military hubs.
recommended_revision: Verify with maps/official records.
reason: No geographic/defence-location source reviewed here.

original_claim: Press coverage may show editorial shifts, contradictions, planted stories, suppression, or deliberate ambiguity.
recommended_revision: Build media corpus separately.
reason: No newspaper corpus coding was performed; claim is an investigative question.

original_claim: Handwriting in the Rubaiyat should be compared with samples from Carl Webb, Jessica Thomson, and other persons of interest.
recommended_revision: Do not present as evidence yet.
reason: Code image can support future handwriting comparison but no comparison samples/results acquired.

original_claim: Carl Webb's pre-death behavior should be reviewed for isolation, travel, relationship breakdown, financial or legal stress.
recommended_revision: Acquire civil, employment, legal records.
reason: No Webb biographical/legal records acquired.

original_claim: Technical skills and limited close ties could have made a person attractive for intelligence recruitment.
recommended_revision: Do not use as fact.
reason: Intelligence recruitment attractiveness is analytical and unsupported by acquired primary sources.

original_claim: AI or machine-learning tools could be applied to the Rubaiyat letter strings for pattern testing.
recommended_revision: Keep as future research method.
reason: Image can support future testing, but no AI/ML analysis protocol or result exists.

original_claim: The case should be compared with Krivitsky, Isdal Woman, Markov, Litvinenko, and other ambiguous or intelligence-linked deaths.
recommended_revision: Revise to: comparative source material for selected related cases exists.
reason: Comparative records for Mangnoson/Marshall were acquired, but no systematic comparison with all named cases was performed.

original_claim: November 30, 1948 was identified as the evening the man was last seen alive.
recommended_revision: Revise to: witnesses reported seeing a man at the site on evening 30 Nov 1948.
reason: Inquest remarks state a man was seen at 7 p.m. on 30 Nov where body was later found; further witness detail in file.

original_claim: December 1, 1948 was identified as the date the body was discovered.
recommended_revision: Use as core chronology fact.
reason: Inquest states body found about 7 a.m. on 1 Dec 1948 at Somerton; death certificate also records date/place.

original_claim: The Tamam Shud note and book were found during December 1948-1949.
recommended_revision: Revise to separate slip discovery from book recovery and mark exact dates pending.
reason: Inquest supports Tamam Shud slip and Rubaiyat link, but exact discovery dates for note and book remain unresolved.

original_claim: Key case locations include Somerton Beach, Jessica Thomson's residence, Adelaide railway station, Woomera Rocket Range, and Maralinga.
recommended_revision: Remove Woomera/Maralinga from key case locations unless direct source found.
reason: Somerton Beach and Adelaide/Henley/Glenelg locations are supported; Woomera and Maralinga are not supported as case locations by primary case records.

original_claim: Leaving a body or message could warn insiders, silence defectors, demonstrate reach, or disrupt rival operations.
recommended_revision: Retain only as hypothesis if at all.
reason: Speculative counterintelligence scenario unsupported by acquired primary sources.

original_claim: Local authorities or intelligence agencies may have deflected focus away from espionage for political reasons.
recommended_revision: Do not state without documentary evidence.
reason: No acquired evidence supports authority deflection from espionage.

original_claim: Carl Webb had a connection to the J.C. Williamson theater company.
recommended_revision: Acquire Webb employment/family records.
reason: No J.C. Williamson/employment source acquired.

original_claim: Tibor Kaldor was a linguist, recent migrant, and had cryptographic skills.
recommended_revision: Acquire records before using.
reason: No Tibor Kaldor biographical source acquired.

original_claim: Tibor Kaldor's death may suggest a broader clandestine network or cleanup operation.
recommended_revision: Do not use as fact.
reason: Speculative Kaldor network/cleanup scenario unsupported.

original_claim: Webb's psychological profile involved trauma, isolation, and artistic temperament.
recommended_revision: Avoid profile claims.
reason: No Webb psychological/biographical primary evidence acquired.

original_claim: Relationships involving Jessica Thomson, Robin Thomson, and others may have created leverage or blackmail opportunities.
recommended_revision: Do not publish without strong evidence.
reason: Speculative leverage/blackmail claim unsupported and sensitive.

original_claim: The case may involve both personal and operational motives.
recommended_revision: Keep only in hypothesis register, not factual claim matrix.
reason: Integrated motive hypothesis unsupported by primary evidence assessment.

original_claim: The case is best understood within a matrix of paranoia, covert rivalry, and operational ambiguity.
recommended_revision: Do not use in evidence-backed dossier until analysis phase.
reason: Analytical summary unsupported as factual claim; requires later synthesis after verification.
